---
title: "because-py"
subtitle: "Python NumPyro backend for Bayesian causal inference"
---

**because-py** is the Python inference engine behind the R [`because`](https://because-pkg.github.io/because/) package.
It translates structural equation models (SEMs) specified as R-style formulas into fully probabilistic
[NumPyro](https://num.pyro.ai/) models and samples them with HMC/NUTS.

You can use **because-py** in two ways:

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### From R via reticulate
Use the familiar `because()` interface in R with `engine = "numpyro"`.
No Python knowledge required.

```r
library(because)

# Simulate data
set.seed(42)
n   <- 200
x   <- rnorm(n)
y   <- 0.7 * x + rnorm(n)
dat <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)

# Fit causal model
fit <- because(
  equations = list(y ~ x),
  data      = dat,
  engine    = "numpyro",
  n.iter    = 1000,
  n.burnin  = 500
)
summary(fit)
```
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Directly in Python
Call `because.fit()` for full control over JAX devices or
integration into Python pipelines.

```python
import because
import numpy as np

# Simulate data
rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
n   = 200
x   = rng.normal(size=n)
y   = 0.7 * x + rng.normal(size=n)

# Fit causal model
result = because.fit(
    equations   = ["y ~ x"],
    data        = {"x": x, "y": y},
    num_samples = 1000,
    num_warmup  = 500
)
```
:::

:::

---

## Installation

See the [Installation & Setup](articles/installation.html) article for full instructions including
JAX/GPU configuration.

```bash
pip install because_py
```

## How it works

because-py parses SEM formulas into a directed acyclic graph (DAG), topologically sorts the
nodes, and generates a NumPyro model closure on the fly. The model is then sampled with NUTS
(No-U-Turn Sampler) via JAX — supporting CPU, GPU, and TPU backends.

For a full description of the statistical framework, see the
[because package documentation](https://because-pkg.github.io/because/).
